name: RoadOS
one_liner: Portable browser-based computer that runs in a single tab.
three_sentence: RoadOS is a compact, browser-based desktop that runs entirely in one tab. It keeps tools, files, and small apps together so you can start working immediately without complex setup. Prototype — features are experimental and we want feedback.
problem: Scattered tools and complex local setups slow people down.
first_click_path: Open RoadOS → Quick tour → Create a workspace
meta_title: RoadOS — Browser computer in one tab
meta_description: RoadOS is a portable, browser-based desktop that keeps your tools and files in one tab. Prototype: try the demo and give feedback.
social_post: Meet RoadOS: a tiny browser computer in one tab. Prototype — try the demo and tell us what’s missing.
overclaim_warning: Prototype — features are experimental; not production-ready.
status_note: HTTP 200; app-like shell detected; functionality not manually verified.

name: RoadCode
one_liner: A simple coding workspace to build, preview, and route projects from your browser.
three_sentence: RoadCode provides an in-browser editor, live preview, and easy routing for small projects and experiments. It focuses on fast feedback and low setup so creators and students can ship prototypes. Prototype stage — integrations are limited and we welcome testing.
problem: Project setup and preview is slow and technical for beginners.
first_click_path: Open RoadCode → Create project → Preview in new tab
meta_description: RoadCode is a lightweight browser-based coding workspace for building, previewing, and routing projects. Prototype — try a quick project.
social_post: RoadCode: code, preview, and share from your browser. Prototype — give it a spin.
overclaim_warning: Prototype — not a full CI/CD system yet.
status_note: HTTP 200; app-like shell detected; functionality not manually verified.

name: RoadTrip
one_liner: A convoy-style space where people and agents collaborate on work together.
three_sentence: RoadTrip is a shared space for humans and the BlackRoad agents to coordinate tasks, suggestions, and decisions. It keeps conversations, agent outputs, and simple workflows together so teams can move faster. Planning stage — designs and agent roles are being defined.
problem: Work flows across many chats and tools, losing context.
first_click_path: Open RoadTrip → See sample convoy → Start a new session
meta_description: RoadTrip is a shared space where people and agents collaborate. Planning stage — help us design the convoy.
social_post: Imagine humans and agents in one shared workspace: RoadTrip. Planning — help shape it.
overclaim_warning: Planning — features are design-stage and not implemented yet.
status_note: HTTP 200; app-like shell detected; functionality not manually verified.

name: PitStop
one_liner: Homework help, tutoring, quizzes, and learning pathways for students and learners.
three_sentence: PitStop provides approachable learning tools: short lessons, quizzes, and guided help from agents. It aims to help students and lifelong learners get unstuck quickly without complex course systems. Planning stage — content models and safety policies are being designed.
problem: Learning resources can be fragmented and hard to follow for beginners.
first_click_path: Open PitStop → Pick subject → Start a short lesson
meta_description: PitStop offers short lessons, quizzes, and friendly tutoring tools to help learners make progress. Planning stage.
social_post: Stuck on homework? PitStop is a friendly place for short lessons and practice. Planning — suggest topics.
overclaim_warning: Planning — not a certified course platform.
status_note: HTTP 200; app-like shell detected; functionality not manually verified.

name: RoadWork
one_liner: Tools for running company operations, approvals, and everyday workflows.
three_sentence: RoadWork provides simple, human-friendly tools for tasks, approvals, and governance flows. It's aimed at small teams that want clear processes without heavy tooling. Planning stage — core flows and permissions are being sketched.
problem: Operations tooling is often complicated and full of jargon.
first_click_path: Open RoadWork → View workflows → Start a simple process
meta_description: RoadWork is a human-friendly toolkit for company operations, approvals, and simple workflows. Planning stage.
social_post: RoadWork: straightforward tools for running small-team operations. Planning — tell us what workflows matter.
overclaim_warning: Planning — not yet shipping operational tooling.

name: BackRoad
one_liner: A simple creator and community surface with real comments and publishing.
three_sentence: BackRoad makes it straightforward to publish content, host discussions, and build community. It prioritizes clear conversations, real comments, and slow-growth community features. Planning stage — community rules and moderation tools are being designed.
problem: Publishing tools are either too complex or too noisy for small creators.
first_click_path: Open BackRoad → Create a space → Publish your first post
meta_description: BackRoad is a clean place to publish work, host comments, and build community. Planning stage.
social_post: BackRoad: publish simply, host real conversations. Planning — help shape the moderation rules.
overclaim_warning: Planning — moderation and publishing features are still being defined.

name: CarKeys
one_liner: Identity and access: device keys, claims, and simple permissions.
three_sentence: CarKeys handles identity, device claiming, and access permissions in a simple, auditable way. It’s built to be understandable — clear ways to claim devices and manage who can do what. Planning stage — security design and user flows are in progress.
problem: Identity tools are confusing and spread across products.
first_click_path: Open CarKeys → Claim a device → Set simple permissions

overclaim_warning: Planning — security flows are under design and review.

name: RoadBook
one_liner: A place for publishing notes, research, peer review, and verified writing.

name: RoadWorld
one_liner: Create walkable rooms, maps, simulations, and small community spaces.
three_sentence: RoadWorld offers simple tools to make small, walkable virtual rooms and maps for communities and games. It focuses on approachable building blocks rather than complex engines. Planning stage — mapping tools and privacy rules are being planned.

name: RoadView

name: RoadChain
one_liner: A simple ledger for provenance, audit trails, and memory receipts.
three_sentence: RoadChain records hashes, receipts, and simple audit trails so teams can verify what happened and when. It’s meant for transparency and basic compliance, not for replacing legal records. Planning stage — storage and privacy safeguards are being designed.
problem: Provenance and audit trails are hard to gather and verify across tools.

name: RoadSide
one_liner: The easiest helpdesk and front door for getting help across BlackRoad.
three_sentence: RoadSide is a simple support surface to route questions and help requests to the right team or agent. It aims to make getting help quick, human, and traceable. Planning stage — routing rules and integrations are being defined.
problem: Finding the right help entry point is often confusing and slow.

name: RoadCoin
one_liner: Internal credits for usage, rewards, and access — not money.
three_sentence: RoadCoin is a non-financial credits layer for contributions, usage, and access control within BlackRoad. It rewards activity and can gate features, but it is not currency for real-world exchange. Planning stage — rules and limits are being worked out.
problem: Tracking and rewarding internal contributions requires a simple, consistent system.
first_click_path: Open RoadCoin → Read model → Try earning a test credit
meta_description: RoadCoin is a credits layer for rewarding contributors and gating features. Not real money. Planning stage.
social_post: RoadCoin: a simple credits system for contributors and usage. Planning — tell us how you'd reward work.
overclaim_warning: Planning — not a currency; no real-world monetary value.

name: OneWay

name: RoadWire
one_liner: News, announcements, feeds, and internal signals for BlackRoad.
three_sentence: RoadWire keeps teams and users informed with clear updates, announcements, and signal channels. It’s designed to be the single source for important status and news. Planning stage — subscriber models and channels are being defined.

name: Detour
one_liner: A safe place for experiments, prototypes, and alternate routes.

problem: Prototypes and experiments often leak into production or lack isolation.
